name: Total Testosterone (T)
summary: Total testosterone measures all testosterone in the blood — the small free fraction plus the portions bound to SHBG and albumin. It is the primary screening test for androgen status, especially when male hypogonadism is being evaluated.
whatItMeans: Testosterone is interpreted alongside the pituitary gonadotropins LH and FSH to localize a problem. Low testosterone with low or inappropriately normal LH/FSH suggests a central (secondary) cause at the hypothalamus or pituitary, while low testosterone with elevated LH/FSH points to a primary testicular cause. Because total T includes protein-bound hormone, a high or low SHBG can pull the total up or down without the biologically active free fraction changing as much. This is educational context, not a diagnosis.
raises: High SHBG states (raises the bound, total fraction)
Resistance training and adequate sleep (modest, physiologic)
lowers: Aggressive caloric deficit / rapid weight loss can transiently lower testosterone
Obesity and insulin resistance (via increased aromatization and lower SHBG)
Chronic opioid use, glucocorticoids, and some other medications
Poor or insufficient sleep and chronic overtraining
Acute illness, major stress, or systemic disease
Pituitary or hypothalamic disorders (lowers via reduced LH/FSH drive)
confounders: Diurnal variation — measure fasting, typically 7–9am when levels peak; afternoon draws read lower
SHBG strongly affects total vs free testosterone, so total can mislead when SHBG is high or low
Recent acute illness, fever, or intense exercise can transiently depress levels
Assay differences — immunoassay vs LC-MS/MS can give different values; harmonized ranges assume a standardized method
Single low values should be confirmed with a repeat morning fasting draw

ranges.note: General/default rule — CDC-harmonized adult-male reference interval (~19–39 y nonobese men), used as the fallback when sex is unknown.
Adult male (CDC Hormone Standardization / harmonized range; basis for ~264–916 ng/dL widely cited by the Endocrine Society).
Older adult male — total testosterone declines with age; example age-banded interval (Mayo/age-specific reporting). Values vary by lab and assay.
Adult female reference interval (Mayo Clinic Laboratories).

references.title: Testosterone, Total, Bioavailable, and Free, Serum (Test TTFB) — Mayo Clinic Laboratories
